The following events are suggestions for ones that might trigger a move to the next stage of the Transformation:
Defeat a powerful force of darkness
Create a hallowed landmark for pilgrims
Establish a parish of worshipers who uphold your virtue
Redeem a soul that was considered beyond redemption
Seraph.entries.0.entries.3.entries.2.ent: When you initially undergo the Seraph Transformation, you gain Celestial Form and one other Stage 1 Boon of your choice. You also gain the Stage 1 Flaw.
Seraph.entries.0.entries.3.entries.3.ent: When you reach Stage 2 of the Seraph Transformation, you select one Stage 2 Boon and gain the Stage 2 Flaw.
Seraph.entries.0.entries.3.entries.4.ent: When you reach Stage 3 of the Seraph Transformation, you select one Stage 3 Boon and gain the Stage 3 Flaw.
Seraph.entries.0.entries.3.entries.5.ent: When you reach Stage 4 of the Seraph Transformation, you select one Stage 4 Boon and gain the Stage 4 Flaw.
Seraph.entries.0.entries.3.name: Seraph Stages
Seraph.name: Seraph
Shadowsteel Ghoul.entries.0.entries.0.en: {@i The cloaked figure hunches over a dusty tome, incanting words of power. At intervals, the figure caresses a dark rod with a sharp point, then jabs themself, causing a sharp gasp of pain. The victim of the figure's curse, sleeping across town, rolls over in his sleep. He will soon suffer the ravages of a Shadowsteel curse. But the curser has a much worse fate awaiting them.}
Shadowsteel Ghoul.entries.0.entries.1: In Etharis, sometimes the metal of the earth itself carries an enormous, vile power. Such is the case with Shadowsteel, a material that has absorbed corrupt power. This makes this substance perfect for warping the magic that passes through it, making it perfect for cursing or enhancing spells.
Shadowsteel Ghoul.entries.0.entries.2: Cursing an enemy, however, requires an enormous amount of work and personal power. These efforts, though enhanced when passing through Shadowsteel, take a toll upon the magic-wielders handling it. By using it, spellcasters and ritualists bind their soul, a bit at a time, to the energies of the fell metal. Those who use Shadowsteel often feel its effects as they move closer and closer to undeath.
Shadowsteel Ghoul.entries.0.entries.3.en: Curses are wrought from and woven with intense feelings of loathing, spite, and bitterness. Humanoids who choose to wield such deeply evil magic leave an indelible mark upon their own soul. When enough of these marks pollute a person, their spirit begins to rot.

A creature who uses a Spellcasting Focus made of Shadowsteel, particularly to cast curses, risks acquiring the Shadowsteel Ghoul curse. Being attuned to a Shadowsteel magic item or wielding a Shadowsteel weapon for a prolonged period also carries a similar danger.

Once the character reaches Stage 1 of the Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation, no typical magic like {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} can remove the affliction. Magic on the level of the {@spell wish} spell is needed to completely remove the Transformation and its Boons and Flaws.
Shadowsteel Ghoul.entries.0.entries.4.en: At each stage of this Transformation, follow the instructions provided for that stage.

To move from one stage to the next higher one in this Transformation, an event or some other notable occurrence tied to the story of the character should take place. The following events are suggestions:
Casting a Shadowsteel curse on a powerful creature that later reaches culmination
Casting a ritual that consumes a Shadowsteel magic item, allowing you to absorb its power
Making a bargain with a Fiend who gifts you with a new object made of Shadowsteel
Casting a 7th-level or higher spell for the first time using a {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24}
Slaying a powerful foe with a weapon forged from Shadowsteel
Failing two {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}
When you initially contract the Shadowsteel Ghoul curse, you select one Stage 1 Boon and gain the Stage 1 Flaw.
When you reach Stage 2 of the Shadowsteel Ghoul curse, you select two Stage 2 Boons and gain the Stage 2 Flaw.
When you reach Stage 3 of the Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation, you gain the Stage 3 Boon and the Stage 3 Flaw.
When you reach Stage 4 of the Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation, you select one Stage 4 Boon and gain the Stage 4 Flaw.

The Shadowsteel Ghoul Transformation might be the most likely change for characters to undertake. Each time a caster uses a {@item Shadowsteel Focus|GrimHollowPG24} to cast a spell, or a warrior wields a Shadowsteel weapon, they risk losing a bit of their soul to gain a bit of power. Players should know what is happening to their characters when they wield Shadowsteel's power. It's a boost to the power of a character, and the risk should be offsetting that power. The toll it takes on the character should be explained by the GM, and hopefully the player—in the spirit of a dark fantasy campaign—should be able to express and illustrate that toll through roleplaying.
Shadowsteel Ghoul.name: Shadowsteel Ghoul
Slaad Host.entries.0: A red slaad implanted a pellet-sized egg in me shortly before I started my adventuring career. If I can't get rid of it in the next two months, the egg will gestate inside me, and a slaad tadpole will burst from my chest, killing me. It will then quickly grow into an adult slaad and kill even more people.
Slaad Host.name: Slaad Host
Specter.entries.0.entries.0.entries.0: {@i The young woman walks the cobblestone streets every evening. No one ever speaks with her. Few even dare to pass her by. She searches for her home, unaware that it's long demolished, and she is long dead. Her haunting image illuminates the dark streets. Her voice echoes in the dusk, "Help me!"}
Specter.entries.0.entries.0.entries.1: {@i A blue figure shambles into town from the graveyard; their body is luminous and ghastly. Their soiled clothes are held together in tatters and their head is marked by twenty gashes. They raise a ghostly hand to point. Their eyes are haunted and filled with rage. The wraith unleashes a cry of the dead that would chill their murderer's blood to ice.}
Specter.entries.0.entries.1: A living person becomes a Specter through death, by contacting the realm of the dead, or through magic that breaks the body's ties to the material world. This Transformation tears at the substance of body and mind, until what is left is a spiritual shadow.
Specter.entries.0.entries.2: Specters have a tenuous grasp on reality, and some fade into oblivion while others become monsters that sow the same. Few cursed with this Transformation have the fortitude to remain whole.
Specter.entries.0.entries.3.entries.0: Methods of becoming a Specter vary, and few find their way willingly. One that dies of an unjust or violent cause may find their spirit refusing to depart, despite their physical form fading. Contact with the forces of death can drain the soul of vitality—a would-be hero is corrupted by the force they wanted to fight. Sinister rituals can also infuse a body with the powers of unmaking from the realm of the Aether Kindred.
Specter.entries.0.entries.3.entries.1: Alongside incorporeal beings and the magic of death stand entities that exist in a reality untethered from the material world as mortals understand it. These others and their minions, manifestations of otherworldly chaos, can infuse a person with that chaos, making the victim's ultimate home a dream of cosmic horror.
Specter.entries.0.entries.3.entries.2.en: Once the character reaches Stage 1 of the Specter Transformation, typical magic like {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} cannot remove the affliction. You cannot be revived or restored by any magic lesser than the {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell due to the ties that anchor your spirit to the corporeal world.
Once you die completely and your soul departs, you may be brought back to life by more conventional magic, free of the Transformation and its Boons and Flaws.
Specter.entries.0.entries.3.name: Becoming a Specter
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.0: At each stage of this Transformation, follow the instructions provided for that stage.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.1.en: To move from one stage to the next higher one in this Transformation, an event or some other notable occurrence tied to the story of the character should take place. Suggestions include:
Defeat a powerful, ghostly Undead and absorb its power.
Defeat an Aberration that has powers over space, time, or death.
Use a ritual that infuses your body with the forces of death or primordial chaos.

Survive a near-death experience caused by a ghostly Undead creature or an Aberration, especially one in which your {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum is reduced, such as by a Wraith's Life Drain, or in which you drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Kill a significant foe with Necrotic or Psychic damage, or by using Necromancy.
Infuse other willing people with the power that corrupts you.
Interact with ghosts and similar spirits, or beings of other realities, perhaps as a conduit between them and other mortals.
Open a gate or path to places of the dead or the dreams of the Aether Kindred.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.2.en: When you initially undergo the Specter Transformation, you gain the Spectral Form Boon and one other Stage 1 Boon of your choice. You also gain the Stage 1 Flaw.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.3.en: When you reach Stage 2 of the Specter Transformation, you select one Stage 2 Boon and gain the Stage 2 Flaw.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.4.en: When you reach Stage 3 of the Specter Transformation, you select one Stage 3 Boon and gain the Stage 3 Flaw.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.entries.5.en: When you reach Stage 4 of the Specter Transformation, you select one Stage 4 Boon and gain the Stage 4 Flaw.
Specter.entries.0.entries.4.name: Specter Stages
Specter.name: Specter
Spirit Medium.entries.0: After a fateful experience, you believe you're aligned with spirits and can serve as a conduit for their insights and goals. You have advantage on any {@skill Arcana} or {@skill Religion} check you make to remember or research information about spirits and the afterlife. Additionally, you begin your adventuring career with a custom-made device for communing with otherworldly forces, perhaps a spirit board, a {@deck tarokka deck|CoS}, an automatic writing planchette, dowsing rods, a cup for tea leaves, or a device of your own design. Add your proficiency bonus to any ability check you make using this type of divining tool.
Spirit Medium.name: Spirit Medium
Spy.entries.0: I'm a spy for the Harpers, a secretive organization that works behind the scenes to thwart tyrants and other evildoers. My mission in Icewind Dale is to safeguard the settlements of Ten-Towns while concealing my Harper affiliation. Credible threats to Ten-Towns include the Arcane Brotherhood, an organization of wizards based in Luskan, and the Zhentarim, an expansive shadow network that wants to control trade throughout the North. Both have threatened Ten-Towns in the past.
Spy.entries.1: My only Harper contact in Ten-Towns is a human named Beldora, who poses as a homeless woman and spends her days gathering information in Bryn Shander.
Spy.name: Spy
Symbiotic Being.entries.0: A second being resides within your body along with you. It manifests physically somewhere on you and relies on you for its survival. At the same time, it offers you knowledge and other assistance, either of its own volition or because of your intertwined nature. The symbiote has its own personality and agenda, which might be at odds with yours. If you ignore the symbiote's desires, it might strive to interfere with you. There is no way to be rid of the symbiote while you have this Dark Gift. Even if you go to drastic lengths, such as severing the part of your body where the symbiote resides, it reappears on your body in another location at the next midnight.
Symbiotic Being.entries.1: Roll on or choose an option from the Symbiotic Nature table to determine what sort of symbiotic being shares your body. Additionally, you gain the traits that follow.
Symbiotic Being.entries.2.caption: Symbiotic Nature
Symbiotic Being.entries.2.rows.0.1: Tiny humanoid facial features protruding from your torso, palm, or the back of your head
Symbiotic Being.entries.2.rows.1.1: An alien appendage inside an unhealing wound
Symbiotic Being.entries.2.rows.3.1: An intrusive thought that inhabits your mind
Symbiotic Being.entries.2.rows.5.1: Crystal growths that replace a portion of your body
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.0.entr: Your symbiote is a separate entity with its own physical form bound to yours. It isn't a separate creature and relies on you to survive. It has Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma scores. The DM sets the symbiote's abilities or determines them randomly (roll {@dice 4d6} for each score, ignoring the lowest roll and totaling the rest). The symbiote can see and hear using your senses.

The symbiote speaks, reads, and understands two languages; one that you speak, as well as one appropriate to its nature. Choose one of the following skills: {@skill Arcana}, {@skill Deception}, {@skill History}, {@skill Intimidation}, {@skill Insight}, {@skill Investigation}, {@skill Nature}, {@skill Religion}, {@skill Perception}, or {@skill Persuasion}. You gain proficiency in that skill if you don't already have it, representing the symbiote's counsel and guidance.
If you die, so does your symbiote. If you are subsequently returned to life, your symbiote revives as well.
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.0.name: Entwined Existence
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.1.entr: Your symbiote has a vested interest in your survival and takes steps to ensure it. When you fail a saving throw, you can choose to have your symbiote expend one of your Hit Dice to roll it and add the number rolled to the saving throw, potentially turning the failure into a success. If it uses this feature on a death saving throw, you succeed on the save and regain 1 hit point regardless of the number rolled on the {@dice d20}.
Once you succeed on a saving throw due to this trait, the symbiote can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.1.name: Sustained Symbiosis
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.2.entr: Your symbiote has an agenda that drives it, and it expects you to assist it in achieving those goals. How permissive or patient it is in resolving its agenda depends on its personality (work with your DM to determine these details). If you have an opportunity to advance its agenda and don't act on it, the symbiote can try to force your hand. You must succeed on a Charisma saving throw ({@dc 12} + the symbiote's Charisma modifier) or be {@condition charmed} by the symbiote for {@dice 1d12} hours. While {@condition charmed}, you must try to follow the symbiote's commands. If you take damage that is not self-inflicted, you can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on a success.
Roll or choose from the Symbiotic Agenda table to determine the symbiote's goal, working with your DM to detail the specifics or create another agenda.
The symbiote seeks to destroy a specific type of being, such as fiends, trolls, or paladins.
The symbiote craves knowledge and is determined to solve a mystery or learn a secret.
The symbiote wants to bring a prophecy to fruition or to thwart one.
The symbiote seeks to defend the servants and interests of a powerful being or organization.
The symbiote wants to keep you from harm at all costs and believes it knows what's best for you.
The symbiote seeks to experience new sensations, the more bizarre the better.
Symbiotic Being.entries.3.entries.2.name: Symbiotic Agenda
Symbiotic Being.name: Symbiotic Being
Touch of Death.entries.0: Your touch is pain, harming whoever you come in contact with. The deathly power within you is beyond your control, afflicting any who touch your bare skin. By the same token, you can deliver death to your enemies with your touch.
Touch of Death.entries.1: Roll on or choose an option from the Deadly Touch table to determine how this Dark Gift presents itself. Additionally, you gain the traits that follow.
Touch of Death.entries.2.caption: Deadly Touch
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.0.1: Your body is alchemically or biologically altered, infusing you with deadly chemicals.
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.1.1: The magic of a slighted druid or fey makes small plants wither and insects die in your presence.
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.2.1: You're the harbinger of a grim prophecy. Any creature your touch damages is marked with a temporary scar of a group, fiend, deity, or other force that takes an interest in you.
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.3.1: You survived a near-death experience—but shouldn't have. Ever since, death and ill omens follow you.
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.4.1: You are out of sync with time or reality. Anything you touch is warped by the distortion.
Touch of Death.entries.2.rows.5.1: Every time you touch a creature, you hear the laughter of someone you once harmed.
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.0.entri: You can focus your deadly touch against your foes. As an action, make one unarmed strike. On a hit, the target takes an additional {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage. This damage increases by {@damage 1d10} when you reach 5th level ({@damage 2d10}), 11th level ({@damage 3d10}), and 17th level ({@damage 4d10}).
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.0.name: Death Touch
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.1.entri: When you hit a target with an attack roll and deal necrotic damage, you ignore the target's resistance to that damage.
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.1.name: Inescapable Death
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.2.entri: When you start your turn grappling a creature or {@condition grappled} by it, the creature takes {@damage 1d10} necrotic damage.
Touch of Death.entries.3.entries.2.name: Withering Contact
Touch of Death.name: Touch of Death
Trauma Survivor.entries.0: You survived. Whatever it was, you made it through, and you can help others do the same. People view you as an expert on the traumatic situation you faced—be it a specific sort of violence, illness, or otherwise. You are aware of nonmagical recovery techniques, common resources, and misinformation. You know how to speak to sympathetic doctors, clergy, and local leaders and can convince them to shelter one person (other than yourself) at a modest lifestyle for up to one month.
Trauma Survivor.name: Trauma Survivor
Traveler.entries.0: You come from somewhere else, a place others couldn't begin to understand. Perhaps your home is a unique corner of the Land of the Mists or another world entirely. In any case, you and other travelers have shared experiences. You can find a place to hide, rest, or recuperate among sympathetic trading caravans, itinerant families, or displaced groups, so long as you don't present yourself as a danger. Such groups will hide you from the law or anyone searching for you, though they won't risk their lives for you. Additionally, you can tell whether an object you can see and touch is from your homeland with near perfect accuracy.
Traveler.name: Traveler
Underdark Experience.entries.0: You are no casual visitor to the Underdark, but instead have spent considerable time there learning its ways. You are familiar with the various races, civilizations, and settlements of the Underdark, as well as its major routes for travel. If you fail an Intelligence check to recall some piece of Underdark lore, you know a source you can consult for the answer unless the DM rules that the lore is unknown.
Underdark Experience.name: Underdark Experience
Unscarred.entries.0: Like the great hero Haktos, you are resistant to physical harm. Use the Unscarred Origin table to determine how you gained your legendary invulnerability. Additionally, you gain the following trait.
Unscarred.entries.1.entries.0.entries.0: When you take damage, you can use your reaction to roll a {@dice d12}. Add your Constitution modifier to the number rolled, and reduce the damage by that total. After you use this trait, you can't use it again until you finish a short or long rest.
Unscarred.entries.1.entries.0.name: Unscarred Resilience
Unscarred.entries.2.caption: Unscarred Origin
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.0.1: I was bathed in hydra's blood as an infant, and my wounds close too quickly to become mortal injuries.
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.1.1: I swallowed a magic stone that fell from Nyx, which made my skin hard as rock.
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.2.1: When I was wounded as a child, a god healed me. Now wounds are reluctant to undo the god's work.
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.3.1: I was rescued from the Underworld, and I'm not sure I fully returned to life—it's always so cold.
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.4.1: I was born on one of the Dakra Isles. All my people are naturally resilient, a fact I didn't realize until I lost my home and ventured into the world.
Unscarred.entries.2.rows.5.1: I don't know who one or more of my parents is, but my resilience (and other hints) provide clues to their immortal identity.
Unscarred.name: Unscarred
Vampire.entries.0.entries.0.entries.0: {@i A twisted figure lurks in the shadows of a castle wall as the roaring infantry charges through a breach. In a flash of black mist, the figure appears in front of the soldiers, one by one, striking them down with inhuman speed.}
Vampire.entries.0.entries.0.entries.1: {@i Candlelight flickers as a hooded advisor leans over an ornate throne, whispering machinations into her lord's ensnared mind. The chamber erupts into commotion as the lord announces further powers be granted to the resident merchants' guild.}
Vampire.entries.0.entries.0.entries.2: {@i A terrified villager flees through the forest, hoping to outrun his pursuer. His eyes burn with tears, sweat, and blood. He stops, listening for the sounds of footsteps behind. His sigh of relief is stopped before it escapes his throat, as fangs sink deep into his neck.}
Vampire.entries.0.entries.1: Vampires are creatures of accursed blood and tragic existence. Exquisite food, a warm summer breeze, and growing old surrounded by loved ones: these are luxuries a Vampire will never experience. Unable to enjoy many of life's pleasures as they once knew them, Vampires often become bitter creatures, filled with hate for everything they once loved.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.2.entries.0: Vampires are spawned into the world when a mortal contracts the Sanguine Curse, dies, and is reborn undead. There are a variety of ways to contract the curse. A Vampire may have offered their blood to a loyal servant, powerful ally, or loved one they wished to elevate. More commonly, a Vampire may bite a victim, who survives the attack long enough to contract the Sanguine Curse before perishing and being reborn.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.2.entries.1: Other methods of becoming a Vampire include ancient and dark magic, as well as powerful but cursed magical artifacts. Vampirism may be passed along when an ancient Vampire allows a beloved plaything to drink blood from the Undead creature's cursed veins. Regardless of how you become a Vampire, you should discuss with your GM what type of Vampire you might become and how it can be implemented in the campaign.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.2.entries.2.en: Once the character reaches Stage 1 of the Vampire Transformation, no typical magic like {@spell Remove Curse|XPHB} can remove the affliction. Magic on the level of the {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell is needed to completely remove the Transformation and its Boons and Flaws.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.2.name: Becoming a Vampire
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.0: At each stage of this Transformation, follow the instructions provided for that stage.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.1.en: To move from one stage to the next higher one in this Transformation, an event or other notable occurrence tied to the story of the character should take place. The following events are suggestions:
Severing a tie to your mortal life, such as discarding a sentimental trinket or abandoning a loved one
Making a further bargain with the Vampire who gifted you with the Sanguine Curse
Drinking the blood of another, more ancient, Vampire
Failing two {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}
Performing a blood ritual using Sangromancy
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.2.en: When you initially contract vampirism, you gain the Fanged Bite Boon and one other Stage 1 Boon. You also gain the Stage 1 Flaw.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.3.en: When you reach Stage 2 of vampirism, you select two Stage 2 Boons and gain the Stage 2 Flaw.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.4.en: When you reach Stage 3 of vampirism, you select two Stage 3 Boons and gain the Stage 3 Flaw.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.5.en: When you reach Stage 4 of vampirism, you gain the Regeneration Boon and one other Stage 4 Boon of your choice. You also gain the Stage 4 Flaw.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.entries.6.en: As a Vampire transforms further and further from its mortal form, its metabolic requirements change. The first change is a need for mortal blood. This need is approximately 1 pint of blood from a Humanoid, Beast, or other creature with mortal blood.

As an action, you can bite a living creature within 5 feet that is {@condition Charmed} by you, or that has the {@condition Incapacitated}, {@condition Paralyzed}, {@condition Restrained}, or {@condition Unconscious} condition. You drain the target of one pint of blood (or similar life-giving substance), leaving a visible bite mark on that creature. The bitten creature gains one {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

A Vampire that does not feed within the required time enters a rabid feeding frenzy under the GM's control until they drain a creature completely (killing them), at which point the Vampire falls unconscious for 4 hours.
Vampire.entries.0.entries.3.name: Vampire Stages
Vampire.name: Vampire
Watchers.entries.0: Something is always watching you and draws ethereal spirits that take the form of creatures made of shadowstuff, usually in the shape of Tiny beasts, that follow you and gather in your general vicinity. These creatures act like normal examples of their kind, but their behavior suggests a hint of malice. No matter what precautions you take, you can't permanently drive these spirits away. They always return, wait just beyond the reach of deterrents, or later crawl out of your clothes and equipment.
Watchers.entries.1: Roll on or choose an option from the Watchers table to determine what sorts of forms the spirits typically take when they follow you.
Watchers.entries.2.caption: Watchers
Watchers.entries.2.rows.1.1: Inescapable Judgments. Animate tools of punishment, drifting eyeballs, religious iconography
Watchers.entries.2.rows.4.1: Unnatural Observers. Animate objects, clockwork devices, otherworldly voyeurs
Watchers.entries.2.rows.6.1: Stray Souls. Ghost orbs, shadows, ectoplasmic wisps
Watchers.entries.3.entries.0.entries.0: As an action, you can influence the presence guiding the watchers for 1 hour. For the duration, you gain advantage on Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) and Wisdom ({@skill Perception}) checks, and you can't be {@condition blinded}. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a long rest.
Watchers.entries.3.entries.0.name: Borrowed Eyes
Watchers.entries.3.entries.1.entries.0: You have disadvantage on Charisma ({@skill Deception}), Charisma ({@skill Performance}), and Charisma ({@skill Persuasion}) checks made against creatures that can see the watchers, and you have disadvantage on saving throws made against the {@spell scrying} spell.
Watchers.entries.3.entries.1.entries.1: You can temporarily disperse or hide the watchers with some effort. With 1 minute of work and a successful {@dc 15} Charisma ({@skill Animal Handling}) check, you can suppress your Borrowed Eyes and Dread Presence for 1 hour. Once you successfully suppress these features, you can't do so again until you finish a long rest.
Watchers.entries.3.entries.1.name: Dread Presence
Watchers.name: Watchers
